query: we're adopting FDD — what are the five processes and which run once vs per feature set?
rationale: core FDD: the five processes and the once-vs-iterative split

query: how should I name features? something like 'calculate the total of an order'?
rationale: FDD feature-naming template <action> the <result> by/for/of/to a(n) <object>

query: how do I report percent-complete per feature with the design and code milestones?
what's the difference between a feature, a feature set, and a major feature set?
who's the chief programmer vs the class owner, and how do feature teams form?
rationale: FDD role structure and dynamically formed feature teams

query: should a feature in our feature list be capped at two weeks, and what if a step is bigger?
rationale: FDD feature sizing rule and decomposition of oversized steps

query: set up Design by Feature and Build by Feature as the iterative inner loop for our team
rationale: FDD processes 4 and 5 as the per-feature-set loop

query: help me establish a ubiquitous language and draw the bounded contexts for our domain model
rationale: near-miss: domain modelling and bounded contexts -> ddd-guide owns Develop-an-Overall-Model substance

query: split this user story into thin vertical slices and check it against INVEST
rationale: near-miss: client-value slicing and INVEST -> user-stories-guide

query: write Given-When-Then scenarios for this feature and run an example-mapping session
rationale: near-miss: Gherkin and example mapping -> bdd-guide

query: what's the right red-green-refactor rhythm for building this feature test-first?
are these classes too coupled — what design quality bar should the code inspection enforce?
rationale: near-miss: design/coupling quality bar -> oop-guide and connascence-guide; FDD only schedules the inspection
